evals.query: Create a Kora ProductController with GET list, GET by id, POST create, PUT update and DELETE routes for products.
expected_behavior: Annotates the class with @Component and @HttpController (no path argument on @HttpController)
Declares each route with @HttpRoute(method = HttpMethod.GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, path = ...) including the full prefix in each path
Binds the id with @Path and the JSON body with @Json on both the method and the body parameter
Returns 201 from create via HttpResponseEntity.of(...) and 204 from delete via HttpServerResponse.of(204, HttpBody.empty())
Does not use foreign-framework REST annotations (e.g. @RestController/@GetMapping/@RequestBody) or field injection; uses constructor injection only

evals.query: I need a Kora search endpoint GET /search/{category} that also reads optional page, size and sort query params plus an optional X-Request-Id header.
expected_behavior: Uses @Path for the category segment (name matching the {category} segment or the argument name)
Uses @Query for page/size/sort and marks the optional ones @Nullable (Kotlin: nullable type)
Uses @Header for X-Request-Id and marks it @Nullable
Does not invent a required=false attribute on @Query or @Header; relies on @Nullable for optionality
Does not use foreign-framework @RequestParam/@PathVariable annotations; uses Kora @Query/@Path

evals.query: Build a Kora POST /orders endpoint that accepts a JSON OrderRequest and returns 201 Created with a Location header and a JSON OrderResponse body.
expected_behavior: Puts @Json on the method and @Json on the OrderRequest body parameter and depends on ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module
Returns HttpResponseEntity.of(201, HttpHeaders.of("Location", ...), response) for the created resource
Defines OrderRequest/OrderResponse as DTOs (records/data classes) annotated @Json
Does not use a fluent HttpServerResponse.ok()/created().body() builder (it does not exist) - uses HttpResponseEntity.of/HttpServerResponse.of

evals.query: In a Kora controller, how do I return a 404 when a user is not found and a 400 on invalid input?
expected_behavior: Throws HttpServerResponseException.of(404, message) when the resource is missing
Throws HttpServerResponseException.of(400, message) for invalid input (or returns an HttpResponseEntity with status 400)
Does not reference fabricated helpers like HttpServerResponseException.notFound()/badRequest()
Does not rely on a foreign-framework @ExceptionHandler/@ControllerAdvice; per-route errors are thrown directly via HttpServerResponseException

evals.query: Add centralized exception handling to my Kora HTTP service that converts exceptions to JSON error responses for all routes.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerInterceptor and registers it globally with @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) and @Component
Continues the chain with chain.process(context, request) and maps errors via .exceptionally(...)
Unwraps CompletionException before inspecting the cause and re-emits HttpServerResponseException with its own code()
Builds the JSON body with a JsonWriter and HttpBody.json(...), returning HttpServerResponse.of(code, body)
Does not use a global @ControllerAdvice-style handler or a non-existent HttpServerExceptionHandler type; handling is done in the interceptor

evals.query: Write a Kora interceptor that logs the method, path, status code and duration of every request.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerInterceptor with intercept(Context, HttpServerRequest, InterceptChain) returning CompletionStage<HttpServerResponse>
Registers globally with @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) and @Component
Returns chain.process(context, request).whenComplete(...) and reads request.method(), request.path() and response.code()
Does not use a fabricated void intercept(request, HttpServerCall) signature or call.proceed(request)

evals.query: How do I apply one interceptor to every route, another to just one controller, and a third to a single route in Kora?
expected_behavior: Uses @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) for the global interceptor and notes only one global interceptor is allowed
Uses @InterceptWith(X.class) on the controller class for the controller-scoped interceptor
Uses @InterceptWith(X.class) on a single @HttpRoute method for the route-scoped interceptor
Describes the execution order as global -> controller -> method -> handler
Does not use a foreign-framework HandlerInterceptor or web-config registration; scope is controlled by @Tag and @InterceptWith

evals.query: Set up the httpServer configuration for a Kora service with separate ports for traffic and metrics, virtual threads, and probe endpoints.
expected_behavior: Sets httpServer.publicApiHttpPort for traffic and httpServer.privateApiHttpPort for metrics/probes separately
Configures privateApiHttpMetricsPath, privateApiHttpReadinessPath and privateApiHttpLivenessPath on the private port
Enables virtual threads with virtualThreadsEnabled = true and notes it requires Java 21+
Plugs in UndertowHttpServerModule on the @KoraApp interface
Does not invent unsupported keys such as httpServer.tls/httpServer.cors blocks

evals.query: I want to build a UserContext object from request headers in a Kora controller without parsing them in every handler.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerRequestMapper<UserContext> and reads values via request.headers().getFirst(...)
Binds the parameter with @Mapping(RequestMapper.class)
Mentions HttpServerResponseMapper + @Mapping for the symmetric custom response case
Does not use a foreign-framework argument resolver; uses HttpServerRequestMapper with @Mapping

evals.query: What Gradle dependencies and annotation processor do I need to start a Kora HTTP server in Java?
expected_behavior: Adds the kora-parent BOM via platform("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent:<version>") and lets all kora artifacts inherit the version
Adds annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" (Kotlin: ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors")
Adds implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:http-server-undertow" and ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module for JSON
Does not version individual ru.tinkoff.kora artifacts and does not use foreign-framework starter dependencies
